overview.name: Popebot — Multi-Channel Agent Communications Layer
overview.description: Every agent gets a communications voice. Popebot routes outbound messages across email, Slack, Twitter, LinkedIn, and SMS on behalf of any agent. All credentials from Infisical — ZERO hardcoded.
overview.integration_point: kernel.py self.popebot — accessible to all agents via kernel.send_comms()
overview.persona: popebot_bishop.soul.md — diplomatic, clear, professional communicator for the entire agent crew

file_structure.archonx/comms/__init__.py: Package init, exports Popebot, Channel, CommMessage
file_structure.archonx/comms/popebot.py: Popebot orchestrator — routes messages to correct channel handler

archonx/comms/channels/.email.py: EmailChannel — SMTP via smtplib + Infisical credentials
archonx/comms/channels/.slack.py: SlackChannel — Incoming Webhook POST (no OAuth needed for MVP)
archonx/comms/channels/.linkedin.py: LinkedInChannel — LinkedIn API v2 Share endpoint

archonx/comms/templates/.daily_digest.txt: Template: daily summary from Pauli King to Bambu

values.NORMAL: Batch with other normal messages (5 min window)

fields.from_agent_id: str — which agent is sending (e.g. pauli_king)
fields.to: str — recipient (email address / slack channel / phone number / @handle)
fields.subject: str | None — email/slack only
fields.body: str — message content (plain text, rendered from template if template_id set)
fields.template_id: str | None — template name in comms/templates/
fields.scheduled_at: datetime | None — if set, queue until this time
fields.sent_at: datetime | None — filled on successful send

fields.external_id: str | None — e.g. email message ID, tweet ID, Twilio SID
fields.retry_after: int | None — seconds to wait before retry

Popebot.description: Central router. Takes CommMessage, picks channel handler, sends, logs result.

constructor.note: vault is archonx/security/vault.py client — all keys loaded from Infisical there

methods.async send(message: CommMessage) -> Comm: Route to correct channel, send, return result
methods.async send_template(from_agent: str, cha: Convenience method — build CommMessage from template then send
methods.async broadcast(from_agent: str, channel: Send same message to multiple channels simultaneously
methods.async queue_digest(message: CommMessage): Defer to LOW priority daily digest batch
methods._get_channel_handler(channel: Channel) -: Return instantiated channel object with credentials

BaseChannel.description: Abstract base for all channel handlers

abstract_methods.validate_recipient(to: str) -> bool: Validate recipient format (email regex, phone E.164, etc)

EmailChannel.implementation: smtplib.SMTP_SSL or SMTP+STARTTLS, MIMEMultipart for HTML emails
EmailChannel.per_agent_from: Each agent sends from their own email: pauli@archonx.io, synthia@archonx.io, etc.

SlackChannel.implementation: httpx.AsyncClient POST to webhook URL with JSON payload
SlackChannel.message_format: Slack Block Kit — attachments with agent color coding

SMSChannel.use_case: CRITICAL priority only — emergency alerts, Bambu direct notifications

TwitterChannel.per_agent_accounts: Each agent has own Twitter account (keys per-agent in Infisical)

infisical_key_naming_convention.note: vault.py already provides get_secret(key) → reads from Infisical. Use that, never os.getenv for secrets.

rate_limiter.overflow_behavior: Queue for next refill window (do not drop), except CRITICAL priority which bypasses

kernel_integration.public_method: async kernel.send_comms(from_agent: str, channel: str, to: str, body: str, priority: str = 'NORMAL') -> CommResult
usage_examples: await kernel.send_comms('pauli_king', 'SLACK', '#archonx-ops', 'Phase 2 complete — all systems green')

daily_digest.description: LOW priority messages batched and sent daily at 08:00 Mexico City time (UTC-6)

test_requirements: test_email_send: Mock SMTP, verify CommMessage fields map to correct MIME fields
test_slack_send: Mock httpx, verify Block Kit JSON format
test_sms_critical_bypass_rate_limit: SMS CRITICAL priority sends despite full bucket
test_rate_limit_queuing: Normal priority blocked by full bucket, queued correctly
test_template_rendering: template_id + vars → rendered body string
test_broadcast: Same message sent to 2 channels simultaneously (asyncio.gather)
test_no_hardcoded_credentials: assert 'password' not in source, assert 'token' not in source
builder_notes: ZERO hardcoded credentials — all tests must use MockVaultClient that returns fake test credentials
For MVP: Email + Slack are priority 1 (most used in practice)
Twitter + LinkedIn are priority 2 (schedule for Phase 2.5 if time is tight)
SMS is priority 3 (Twilio setup needs account — flag as 'needs human Twilio SID' if blocked)
httpx must be used for async HTTP (not requests — requests is sync)
All channel handlers must handle network errors gracefully (timeout=10s, one retry)
Log every send attempt to archonx/data/comms_log.jsonl (append-only audit trail)
